emergency_definition: Emergency calls from regular property managers (e.g., gas station pump failures)
Emergency calls from regular general contractors

emergency_routing_rules.fallback_action: Take message, Ben will call back

non_emergency_routing_rules.action: Take message and notify Ben via email and text
non_emergency_routing_rules.message: Standard calls: Clara takes info, Ben calls back; some key contacts ring through directly

integration_constraints: Uses Jobber as CRM — direct integration not yet available
Jobber handles scheduling, invoicing, and customer records
Manual entry from Clara dashboard to Jobber required for now
after_hours_flow_summary: Ben is the only on-call person. Takes emergency calls from regular contractors and property managers. Non-emergency callers after hours are told to call back during business hours or leave a message.
office_hours_flow_summary: Clara answers all calls during business hours. Takes caller info and notifies Ben. Some whitelisted contacts (key contractors, wife) ring through to Ben directly. Ben calls back non-whitelisted callers.
questions_or_unknowns: Exact business hours (start/end times) not confirmed during demo
Timezone not explicitly stated (assumed Calgary = Mountain Time)
Secondary emergency contact not identified (operations manager mentioned but not named)
Complete list of whitelisted direct-ring contacts not finalized
notes: Ben is a sole proprietor with 30 years of electrical experience, running his own business for 8 years. Currently has 3 journeymen, 1 apprentice, and uses subcontractors for overflow. Bringing on an operations manager. Previously tried a virtual assistant (real person) and a business phone system, both unsuccessfully. Uses Jobber CRM. Gets 20-50 calls/week. Signed up for the starter plan ($249/mo, 500 minutes, 3-month trial). Does not do gemstone lights. Service area is Calgary-based.
